name: Jordan Pack
signatureMoves: TOUR OF THE WOODS (tour of the islands)
whyImHere:
Jordan Pack did not come to OWL for fame, championships, or acceptance. Known only as “The Mad Wolf,” Pack emerged from the woods of Durango, Colorado, where rumors claim he was raised among wolves. Whether those stories are true remains unknown—and Pack has never cared enough to explain himself.

A loner by nature, he enters the ring wearing a wolf headdress and a straitjacket, unleashing years of frustration through overwhelming strength and brutal violence. Pack displays the instincts of a predator: patient, territorial, relentless, and always ready to strike. Wrestling gives him a place to release the rage that has followed him out of the wilderness. He is not here to belong. He is here to hunt.

name: Errol Capleton
whyImHere:
Errol Capleton carries the rhythm, pride, and fighting spirit of Jamaica everywhere he goes. Known as “Bad Mon” and celebrated by many as the Pride of Jamaica, Capleton enters OWL determined to represent his homeland on the biggest stage possible. His energy is infectious, but opponents quickly learn that the smile disappears once the bell rings.

Capleton relies on explosive speed, sharp instincts, and unpredictable strikes that can end a match before his opponent realizes they are in danger. One moment he is dancing just outside their reach; the next, they are staring up at the lights. He fights with confidence, heart, and the belief that every victory belongs not only to him, but to everyone back home cheering his name.

name: Killian Crowe
whyImHere:
Killian Crowe did not come to OWL seeking attention. Attention inevitably finds him. Operating from the shadows, Crowe studies every opponent, every weakness, and every careless decision before choosing the perfect moment to strike. Nothing he does is accidental; every movement carries purpose, and every victory is another piece of a design only he fully understands.

Crowe refuses to be defined as hero, villain, or anything so ordinary. He considers himself an enigma—an independent force bound to no cause and controlled by no one. OWL offers stronger competition, greater influence, and a larger stage upon which to expose those who mistake mystery for weakness. By the time his opponents understand Killian Crowe, it is already far too late.

name: Nick Stone
whyImHere:
Nick Stone earned the name “Hard to Kill” the hard way. Across the independent circuit, he built a reputation for surviving punishment that would finish anyone else—and then finding another gear when the match mattered most. Stone brings rare flair to the ring, blending speed, power, and showmanship into a style that is impossible to ignore.

He is flashy because he has earned the right to be. Every pose, taunt, and confident grin is backed by the ability to deliver once the bell rings. Stone does not confuse confidence with arrogance; he simply knows exactly how good he is. After becoming a favorite everywhere he competed, he came to OWL to prove that his success was never limited to the independents. Bigger stage, tougher opponents—same result.

name: Ronin Fairchild
whyImHere:
Ronin Fairchild arrived in OWL with the confidence of a man who has never been told no—and the pedigree to make sure no one forgets it. Hailing from Chelsea and raised in wealth, privilege, and refinement, Fairchild presents himself as the perfect gentleman: polished, educated, and convinced he is the standard everyone else should be judged against.

But beneath the manners and sophistication is a ruthless competitor who will bend the rules whenever it serves him. Like any true aristocrat, Fairchild believes winning is simply his birthright. Backed by elite training money could buy and a smug certainty that he is physically and mentally superior, he steps into OWL to prove that class, skill, and status still reign above all. In Ronin Fairchild’s world, excellence should always look expensive.

name: Mack Aleven
whyImHere:
Mack Aleven knows what it means to come from a place where people expect your story to end before it ever really begins. Raised in the hard streets of southwest Atlanta, “Bulletproof” once lived with the kind of energy, confidence, and street mentality that made him respected—but also nearly cost him everything. After catching a gun charge, Aleven turned to wrestling as his way out, trading the streets for the ring without losing the swagger that made him who he is.

At just 5'6", he gets overlooked until the bell rings. Then the underestimation disappears fast. Mack moves with explosive agility, fearless instincts, and the sharp confidence of a young street general who refuses to back down. He is here to prove that survival made him dangerous—but wrestling made him unstoppable.

name: Riley King
whyImHere:
Riley King carries himself like a man who already knows where he belongs—at the top. Known as “The Golden Child,” King believes his years on the independent scene have prepared him to stand among the best young wrestlers in the industry. He is not chasing hype or living off potential. He sees himself as the next great standard-bearer, built on skill, confidence, and a bloodline of wrestling excellence reflected in his kingly identity.

In the ring, King combines sharp technique, quick instincts, and the kind of polished all-around ability that makes him dangerous against anyone. But for all the talent and pedigree, his purpose is simple: win. Riley King came to OWL for one thing and one thing only—gold. If greatness runs in his lineage, then championships are his destiny.

name: Jayden Alfah
whyImHere:
Jayden Alfah grew up seeing wrestling and anime the same way: as stories about people refusing to stay down when the world tells them they have reached their limit. Whenever life knocked him off course, he treated it like another chapter—not the ending. That mindset followed him into wrestling, where every setback became training, every rival became a new obstacle, and every match became part of his climb toward something greater.

Inspired by the intensity and imagination of his favorite wrestler, Kenny Omega, Jayden developed a style built around explosive offense, dramatic momentum shifts, and relentless resolve. He calls himself “Omega Level” because he believes there is always another transformation waiting inside him. Jayden came to OWL to prove that main-character determination is not fantasy—it is what happens when someone refuses to quit before reaching the final chapter.

name: Allen Rain
whyImHere:
Rain Allen was born in Toronto, but everything about him feels like a warning from the sky before disaster hits. Known as “The Storm Bringer,” Allen carries himself with quiet focus until the bell rings—then the calm disappears. His style is sudden, sharp, and unpredictable, built on fast strikes, explosive movement, and the ability to change the pace of a match without warning.

Rain does not need to shout to make people feel his presence. He believes pressure reveals the truth: who can survive, who will break, and who was never ready for the storm in the first place. OWL gives him the perfect place to test that theory. Every opponent steps into the forecast. Rain Allen decides how bad the damage gets.

name: Fabian Atlas
whyImHere:
Fabian Atlas does not enter OWL like an ordinary wrestler. He arrives like a monument brought to life. Known as “The Greek Physique,” Atlas has been called a man chiseled from marble, built with the strength, posture, and presence of something out of myth. But beneath the godlike image is a competitor who believes power should be earned, controlled, and used with purpose.

Atlas carries himself with pride, not arrogance. He sees the ring as his arena, the fans as his city, and every opponent as another trial placed before him. His strength is not just for show; he uses it to overwhelm, ground, and subdue anyone reckless enough to test him. Fabian Atlas came to OWL to prove that legends are not only remembered from the past. Some are still being built.

name: Percy Franklin
whyImHere:
Percy Franklin did not come up through bright lights and clean opportunities. He was built in back rooms, basement fights, late-night deals, and places where respect had to be taken before it was ever given. Known as “The Underground Boss,” Franklin carries himself like a man who already controls the room before he walks into it.

He is smooth, calculated, and dangerous—equal parts street general and fight promoter, the kind of man who understands that power is not always loud. Sometimes it is quiet, patient, and waiting for the right moment to collect. Percy sees OWL as a new territory, and every opponent as someone standing in the way of expansion. He is not here to play wrestler. He is here to build an empire, one body at a time.

name: Kwame Mensah
whyImHere:
Kwame Mensah was never meant to be the face of OWL’s future. He was supposed to be insurance. When whispers spread that a major signing could change the balance of power, someone behind the scenes went searching for a weapon they believed they could influence, control, and point in the right direction.

What they found in Accra, Ghana, was something far more dangerous.

Known as “The Gold Coast King,” Mensah is a giant with conquest in his blood and world domination on his mind. He does not see OWL as a workplace. He sees it as territory waiting to be claimed. The people who brought him in may think they hold the leash, but Kwame believes kings answer to no one. Now that he is here, OWL has to find out whether it signed a protector—or unleashed a tyrant.

name: Paddy Saints
whyImHere:
Paddy Saints earned the name “Bad Luck” long before he ever stepped into OWL. Back in Ireland, he was known as the thug for the pub—the man you called when talking was finished and someone needed to be dealt with. Street fights made his reputation, and by those standards, Paddy was damn near unbeatable.

Now he has come to the States to rep the green, make some green, and follow the hard-hitting path carved by men like Sheamus and Fit Finlay. The American wrestling style has not come easy, and Paddy is still learning how to turn chaos into clean victories. But win or lose, one thing never changes: if you run into Paddy Saints, your night is going to end badly. That is why they call him Bad Luck.

name: Travis Cudi
whyImHere:
Travis Cudi does not come to OWL polished, packaged, or pretending to be anything he is not. He comes straight out of Texas with crashout energy, a mean streak, and a reputation built on making fights ugly fast. Known as “The Lone Star Killer” and “Mr. Belt 2 Ass,” Cudi walks into every match like somebody owes him money, respect, or both.

He is not here to wrestle pretty. He is here to bring Texas violence to anyone standing across from him. Cudi has already made noise on the independent scene, including being promoted as the MPX Prospect Champion and appearing for Texas-based events under that same Lone Star Killer identity.

OWL gives him a bigger stage, but the mission stays simple: talk less, swing first, and leave people remembering exactly what happens when Travis Cudi takes off the belt.

name: Atua Tupu
whyImHere:
Every wrestling promotion needs a Samoan force. OWL did not just get one—they got a damn god.

Atua Tupu arrived carrying the weight of island pride, ancestral power, and a presence that feels bigger than the ring itself. Known as “The Samoan God,” Tupu wrestles like a storm given human form: heavy hands, crushing power, and a calm brutality that makes every movement feel inevitable. He does not rush. He does not panic. He simply advances, and opponents either move or get broken.

But Atua is not just here to be feared. He believes his bloodline carries a responsibility to dominate, protect legacy, and prove that greatness is not claimed through words—it is carved into people. OWL wanted a Samoan. What it received was something closer to a prophecy.

name: Chris Danger
whyImHere:
Chris Danger is proof that sometimes a created wrestler becomes bigger than the screen that introduced him. What started as a custom character on DenkOps’ WWE 2K MyCareer series turned into a full-blown wrestling identity, followed by fans who watched Danger go from digital underdog to real-life competitor. The character first broke through during WWE 2K15 content, eventually becoming a staple of DenkOps’ wrestling universe.

But Danger did not stay fictional. Chris Denker trained for the ring, debuted in 2021, and brought the name to life in front of real crowds. OWL gives him another chance to blur that line between fantasy and reality. He is here to prove this was never just a gimmick, a game, or an internet story. Chris Danger is here because the fans created the legend—but now he has to prove the legend can survive.

name: Kai Valentino
whyImHere:
Kai Valentino was never interested in blending in. Born and raised in Miami, he learned early that confidence could be armor, identity could be power, and attention was only dangerous if you did not know what to do with it. As OWL’s first openly gay wrestler, Kai does not carry that history quietly. He wears it with pride, style, and the kind of presence that makes every entrance feel like an event.

Known as “The Main Attraction,” Valentino believes wrestling needs more than toughness. It needs charisma, courage, and someone bold enough to make the spotlight follow them. Miami gave him flash, rhythm, and attitude, but the ring gave him purpose. Kai came to OWL to prove he is not here as a symbol or a side story. He is here to win, shine, and make sure nobody looks away.

name: Mascara Mota
whyImHere:
Mascara Mota was born in Tijuana, where the lucha libre tradition is loud, fast, colorful, and impossible to fake. Known as “El Lechuga Diablo,” Mota built his name by mixing classic high-flying lucha with a laid-back, unpredictable personality that keeps opponents guessing. He may walk in smiling, vibing, and acting like nothing bothers him, but once the bell rings, he becomes a blur.

They call him “The HIIIIIGH Flyin’” for a reason. He believes wrestling should feel like a party, a fight, and a magic trick all at once. Inspired by Tijuana’s wild energy and his own cannabis-fueled creativity, he turns every match into controlled chaos. His 420 Splash does not just end matches—it leaves opponents wondering what the hell just landed on them. Mascara Mota came to OWL to elevate lucha, elevate himself, and stay higher than the competition.

name: Cameron "Cam" Flash
whyImHere:
Cameron “Cam” Flash talked himself into OWL before he ever wrestled his way in. Known online as Mr. 4K @Cam_FlashTV, he built his name as an annoying streamer, loudmouth wrestling critic, and proud member of the internet wrestling community—the kind of guy who watched one match and immediately claimed he could do it better. After one dare too many, Cam stepped into a ring to prove the internet right. The problem? The internet may have lied.

Flash is flashy, obnoxious, and convinced every match is content. His nickname sounds elite, but “4K” is also the follower count he cannot seem to break past. Most of his offense is questionable at best: mistimed dives, ugly landings, and moves that only work when gravity feels generous. But every now and then, Cam catches someone slipping and turns a botch into an opportunity. He may not be good yet—but he is loud enough to make OWL notice.

name: Ren Takeda
whyImHere:
Ren Takeda was born in Tokyo, but his legend was shaped in the quiet hours after the crowds went home. While others chased fame, Takeda trained in silence, obsessed with becoming something rare: a wrestler with the patience of a predator, the precision of a fighter, and the spirit of a warrior who never wastes motion.

Known as “The Golden Tiger,” Takeda carries himself with calm intensity until the bell rings. Then the stillness disappears, replaced by sudden strikes, explosive movement, and a killer instinct hidden behind his composed expression. The tiger is not loud because it does not need to be. It waits, watches, and attacks when the opening appears.

Takeda came to OWL to prove that greatness does not always roar. Sometimes it stalks quietly from Tokyo, wrapped in gold, until the whole world realizes it is already too late.

name: Lio Rush
whyImHere:
Lio Rush has always been faster than the room could understand, but Blackheart is something different entirely. The man once known for flash, confidence, and impossible speed has evolved into something darker—less interested in attention and more interested in control. Every twitch, crawl, strike, and sudden burst of offense feels designed to make opponents uncomfortable before they ever get hurt.

As ROH World TV Champion, Rush brings championship credibility with a mind that no longer operates by normal rules. He is still explosive, still brilliant, still capable of ending a match in a blink—but now there is something haunting behind the eyes. Blackheart Lio Rush came to OWL because chaos travels well, fear spreads fast, and no one is ever truly ready for what he has become.

name: Ilja Dragunov
whyImHere:
Ilja Dragunov does not wrestle like a man trying to win. He wrestles like a man trying to survive something only he can see. Known as “The Mad Dragon” and Unbesiegbar, Dragunov built his reputation on violence, endurance, and an almost uncomfortable willingness to absorb punishment just to give it back harder. His wars across NXT UK, NXT, and WWE proved that pain is not something he avoids—it is something he weaponizes. He is a former NXT UK Champion and NXT Champion, with a career defined by intensity and championship-level credibility.

In OWL, Dragunov is not here for theatrics or shortcuts. He is here to test the limits of every opponent placed in front of him. Every chop, suplex, headbutt, and Torpedo Moscow carries the same message: Ilja Dragunov can be hurt, but he cannot be broken.

name: Kyle Fletcher
whyImHere:
Kyle Fletcher is no longer just the talented young half of a great tag team. He is “The Protostar,” a future star that stopped waiting for permission to explode. In AEW, Fletcher’s transformation through the Don Callis Family turned him colder, sharper, and far more dangerous, choosing ambition over friendship and proving he was willing to betray even close allies to climb higher. AEW has presented him as a major Callis Family weapon, including his pursuit of the AEW International Championship against former ally Konosuke Takeshita.

Fletcher brings size, speed, and a nasty edge that makes him dangerous in any kind of match. He is not in OWL to be compared to Will Ospreay, Aussie Open, or anyone from his past. He is here to prove the Protostar is not potential anymore. It is impact, ego, violence, and gold waiting to happen.

name: Calvin Tankman
whyImHere:
Calvin Tankman is proof that heavyweight wrestling does not have to move slow. Known as “Heavyweight Hustle” and “Indestructible,” Tankman brings the presence of a classic powerhouse with the surprise factor of someone who can strike fast, move quick, and hit offense most opponents never expect from a man his size. He has built his name across the independent scene, MLW, and DPW, where he became the inaugural DPW National Champion.

In OWL, Tankman is not here to be treated like a special attraction or a novelty big man. He is here to remind people that power, speed, and violence can live in the same body. Every opponent has a plan until Tankman starts walking them down. Then it becomes simple: survive the weight, survive the hands, survive the hustle.

name: Bandido
whyImHere:
Bandido arrives in OWL carrying the aura of a world-traveled luchador who has already proven he can thrive anywhere. AEW officially lists him as part of Brodido and as ROH World Champion, giving him the kind of championship credibility that instantly makes him more than just another masked high-flyer.

But Bandido’s danger is not only in the gold around his waist. It is in the blend: lucha speed, wild strength, fearless risk-taking, and the confidence of a man who has stood across from elite competition and never looked out of place. AEW itself has framed him as one of wrestling’s best, with a 2026 Revolution preview saying he made a case for being the best wrestler of 2025.

In OWL, Bandido is here to prove that honor, power, and spectacle can all wear the same mask.

name: Finn Balor
whyImHere:
Finn Bálor arrives in OWL as a man who has already lived several careers in one lifetime. Before WWE, before NXT, before becoming the first-ever Universal Champion, Bálor sharpened himself across Ireland and Japan, building the precision, discipline, and danger that made him impossible to ignore.

But Finn is not only technique and legacy. There is always something darker beneath the surface. Whether he walks in as the calm, calculating veteran or lets the Demon’s shadow creep closer, Bálor understands how to control a room before the fight even begins.

In OWL, he is not here to prove he belongs. That part is history. Finn Bálor is here to remind everyone that legends can still evolve—and still bite.

name: Yoshiki Inamura
whyImHere:
Yoshiki Inamura is not built like a visitor. He is built like someone OWL has to survive. A NOAH powerhouse with a background in judo and sumo, Inamura developed a reputation as a hard-hitting heavyweight prospect whose strength was sharpened through serious Strongman training.

After taking his fight overseas through Europe and WWE NXT, Inamura returned to Japan tougher, broader, and more dangerous, eventually capturing the GHC Heavyweight Championship and defending it through a hard-fought reign.

In OWL, Inamura brings the spirit of a classic Japanese monster ace: disciplined, physical, proud, and impossible to move once he plants his feet. He does not need flash to intimidate anyone. He lets the impact speak. Every strike, throw, and collision carries the same warning—Yoshiki Inamura came to test the foundation of this company.

name: Mike Bailey
whyImHere:
“Speedball” Mike Bailey arrives in OWL with a reputation built on movement, precision, and controlled violence. From Montreal to the international scene, Bailey became known for a striking style that feels almost impossible to time: lightning-fast kicks, sudden angles, and the kind of footwork that can make even elite opponents look a step behind. AEW lists him from Montreal, Quebec, Canada, and presents him under the “Speedball” identity.

But Bailey is more than flash. He has battled through major title scenes in AEW, including a World Championship challenge against Darby Allin and wars tied to JetSpeed’s rise and collapse. In OWL, Bailey is here to prove that speed is not a gimmick. It is pressure, timing, discipline, and impact delivered before the opponent can even react.

name: Ricky Sosa
whyImHere:
Ricky Sosa arrives in OWL with the kind of momentum that cannot be manufactured. Billed from Brussels, Belgium, Sosa turned heads as a European standout before becoming a viral sensation, powered by the crowd chanting “Bang, bang, Sosa” right along with him. TNA now lists him on its official roster, and his signing followed a breakout run that included his televised TNA debut and the buzz around his Blue Thunder Bang finisher.

But Sosa is not here just because the internet found him. He is here because the talent backs up the noise. Young, athletic, flashy, and fearless, Sosa wrestles like someone who knows the spotlight can disappear if you do not seize it. OWL is another stage, another crowd, and another chance to prove the chant was never hype—it was a warning.

name: Leon Slater
whyImHere:
Leon Slater does not wrestle like someone waiting for his time. He wrestles like someone who decided his time started already. Known as “The Youngest in Charge,” Slater came out of the UK scene with freakish athleticism, fearless confidence, and one of the most spectacular arsenals in wrestling. His rise in TNA made history when he became the youngest X-Division Champion ever at just 20 years old.

But Slater is more than a highlight reel. He has represented TNA during its crossover with WWE/NXT, defended the X-Division Title on NXT, and carried himself like a young star trusted with big moments before most wrestlers even find their footing. In OWL, Slater brings the Swanton 450, sky-high confidence, and the attitude of a man who is not chasing the future—he is already living in it.

name: Will Kroos
nickname: He's big, he's bad, he'll bodyslam your dad
whyImHere:
Mason Rook did not arrive in NXT quietly. Formerly known as Will Kroos on the British independent scene, Rook made his WWE presence felt by attacking NXT Champion Tony D’Angelo and immediately proving he was not built to wait in line. WWE presents him as a massive, dangerous force with surprising athletic ability, including one of the biggest moonsaults NXT has seen.

In OWL, Rook brings that same wrecking-ball energy: too big to ignore, too unpredictable to safely game-plan for, and too ambitious to be treated like just another newcomer. He has carried himself through PROGRESS, NOAH, and NXT like a man who knows his size is only part of the problem. Mason Rook is here to crush expectations, silence doubters, and prove that the new monster on the board moves however he wants.

name: Will Komplane
whyImHere:
Will Komplane believes wrestling has been ruined by everyone except him. As the self-appointed voice of The I.W.C., Komplane treats every match, promo, entrance, and mistake like evidence in a never-ending online argument. He does not just want to win—he wants to correct the entire industry.

Loud, smug, and impossible to satisfy, Will built The I.W.C. as a crusade against what he calls “bad booking, bad taste, and bad fans.” In reality, he is an arrogant control freak who thinks his opinions are facts and his complaints are wisdom. As the leader, Komplane does the talking, the judging, and the finger-pointing. OWL gives him a platform, and he plans to use it to shame the roster into his version of perfection.

name: Buzz Kihl
whyImHere:
Buzz Kihl is the reason people stop laughing when The I.W.C. walks into the room. While Will Komplane runs his mouth, Buzz handles the consequences. He is the muscle, the enforcer, and the blunt-force punctuation mark at the end of every complaint.

Buzz does not need long speeches or clever insults. He believes the best way to prove a point is to put someone through the mat and let Will explain why they deserved it afterward. As part of The I.W.C., Buzz acts like he is “cleaning up” OWL, but the truth is simpler: he likes hurting people under the cover of moral superiority. Komplane gives the orders, Buzz delivers the damage, and together they plan to censor anyone who does not fit their standards.

name: Levi Martín
whyImHere:
Levi Martin grew up with every advantage money could buy, but comfort bored him. He did not need wrestling to survive—he chose it because he wanted something that could finally push back. That search led him to Syrup City Wrestling Academy, where privilege met punishment and Levi learned that talent means nothing if you cannot take a hit.

At SCWA, he found Connor Canada, and together they became True North Elite: two Canadians with different backgrounds but the same hunger for control, respect, and championship gold. Levi brings the polish, arrogance, and cold confidence of a man used to getting what he wants. He is not here to prove he belongs with the common man. He is here to prove he was born above them.

name: Connor Canada
whyImHere:
Connor Canada was supposed to be a great hockey goalie. He had the talent, the reflexes, and the toughness—but he also had a problem: he could not stop fighting people. On the ice, off the ice, after whistles, before whistles, Connor always found a reason to drop the gloves. When your goalie is skating across the rink looking for smoke, something has gone very wrong.

Syrup City Wrestling Academy gave him a place to turn that anger into something useful. There, he met Levi Martin, and the two became True North Elite, tearing through the Canadian indies with a mix of privilege, violence, and championship ambition. Connor is the mean streak of the team: rough, angry, physical, and proud of it. OWL is just the next arena, and he still has no interest in staying in his crease.

name: Ricochet
whyImHere:
Ricochet comes to OWL with a reputation that speaks before he ever leaves his feet. AEW lists him as part of The Demand, and that name fits the version of Ricochet standing in front of the world now: sharper, colder, and no longer interested in being admired only for highlights. He wants control, attention, and proof that the same people who praised his athleticism should have feared his ambition too.

He has been called one of the most spectacular athletes in wrestling for a reason, but Ricochet is not here to be anyone’s human GIF. He is a former inaugural AEW National Champion, winning the title in a Casino Gauntlet at Full Gear, and he brings that same opportunistic edge to OWL. Every flip, strike, and sudden counter is no longer just beautiful—it is a warning. Ricochet is here to remind everyone that gravity was never his limit. It was everyone else’s.

name: Johnny Gargano
whyImHere:
Johnny Gargano has spent his career proving that being smaller never meant being lesser. Built on heart, precision, and the refusal to stay down, Gargano came to OWL looking for a new mountain to climb. But this time, he did not want just another singles chase. He wanted a partner who could match his obsession for wrestling at its purest level.

That led him to Zack Sabre Jr. At first, they did not like each other much. Johnny saw Zack as cold and arrogant. Zack saw Johnny as emotional and dramatic. Then they got in the ring together and realized the same thing: nobody else thinks this deeply about wrestling. Gargano brings the heart, pace, and resilience. Zack brings the cruelty, control, and technique. Together, they are chasing one goal: to prove great wrestling still wins.
team: B.T.T.I.T.W (Best Tag Team In The World)

name: Zack Sabre Jr.
hometown: The Isle of Sheppey in Kent, England
whyImHere:
Zack Sabre Jr. does not believe in wasted motion, wasted emotion, or wasted opponents. He sees wrestling as a problem to be solved, a body to be dismantled, and a mind to be embarrassed before the bell ever stops ringing. When he came to OWL, he had no interest in friendship, nostalgia, or playing nice with the roster.

Then Johnny Gargano became useful.

Zack respected Johnny’s toughness before he respected the man himself. Gargano kept surviving positions most wrestlers would panic in, kept adapting, and kept forcing Zack to think one step further. That interested him. Zack still thinks Johnny is too sentimental, too dramatic, and entirely too American about things—but he also knows Gargano can fight, endure, and create openings. For Zack, that makes him a rare kind of partner: one worth tolerating.
team: B.T.T.I.T.W (Best Tag Team In The World)

name: Brody King
whyImHere: Brody King brings chaos with him everywhere he goes. Massive, brutal, and completely unbothered by pain, he fights like a man who enjoys turning matches into disasters. There’s an aura around Brody that makes opponents uneasy before the bell even rings, and once it does, that feeling usually gets worse. He doesn’t just want to beat people — he wants to overwhelm them, break their spirit, and leave a lasting impression. As one half of Black Tide, Brody is the storm itself: violent, suffocating, and impossible to stop once he gets rolling. OWL isn’t ready for the damage he plans to do.

name: Jeff Cobb
whyImHere: Jeff Cobb didn’t come to OWL to fit in — he came to run through people. A world-class powerhouse with freak athleticism, Cobb blends raw island strength with explosive speed that few men his size should even have. He built his name by ragdolling opponents, throwing them across the ring, and making violence look effortless. But on his own, Cobb is dangerous. Aligned with Brody King, he becomes something worse. In Black Tide, Cobb is the engine of destruction: a relentless force who crushes resistance and drowns anyone standing in the way. He’s here for domination, plain and simple.

name: Gabe Kidd
whyImHere:
Gabe Kidd does not wrestle like a man looking for respect. He wrestles like someone looking for a receipt. Loud, violent, reckless, and proud of every scar he leaves behind, Kidd came to OWL with the kind of reputation that makes promoters nervous and opponents check the exit before the bell rings.

Joining Bounty Hunnaz gave him something he always understood: a target. Gabe does not need a championship speech or some grand mission. Point him at somebody, give him a reason, and he will turn the match into a crime scene. He is not here to play wrestler. He is here to collect debts, break mouths, and make sure every team in OWL learns that running from Gabe Kidd only makes the hunt more fun.

name: Jacob Fatu
whyImHere:
Jacob Fatu is not the kind of man you hire unless the situation has already gotten ugly. A destroyer with terrifying speed, violent instinct, and bloodline power behind every strike, Fatu does not just beat opponents—he overwhelms them. He is the kind of force that makes a team feel outnumbered even when the numbers are even.

In Bounty Hunnaz, Jacob is the nightmare at the end of the chase. Gabe Kidd brings the chaos, but Fatu brings the final consequence. Once he locks onto a target, there is no escape route, no safe corner, and no clever plan that survives contact. OWL did not bring him in for peace. It brought him in because some people only understand fear when it has a face.

name: Rush
whyImHere:
RUSH does not enter OWL looking for permission, approval, or friendship. El Toro Blanco walks in like every room already belongs to him, and if anyone disagrees, he is more than happy to make the conversation physical. Violent, proud, and impossible to intimidate, RUSH brings the kind of pressure that makes opponents feel trapped before the bell even rings.

In Los Ingobernables: Plata o Plomo, RUSH is the force that sets the tone. He does not negotiate softly. He does not chase respect. He takes it, breaks it, and leaves the rest of the roster to decide whether they want to pay tribute or pay the price. OWL wanted stars with presence. RUSH brings conquest.

name: Andrade
whyImHere:
Andrade enters OWL with elegance, arrogance, and danger wrapped into one man. He is too polished to look desperate, too talented to be ignored, and too proud to settle for being treated like anyone else. Every movement carries purpose, from the way he walks to the way he punishes an opponent for one mistake.

As part of Los Ingobernables: Plata o Plomo, Andrade is the cold hand behind the threat. Where RUSH brings the storm, Andrade brings calculation. He knows when to strike, when to manipulate, and when to let someone think they are safe right before taking everything from them. OWL is not just another stop for Andrade. It is another empire waiting to be dressed in gold.

name: Dragon Lee
whyImHere:
Dragon Lee has built his name on speed, precision, and fearless lucha excellence, but Double Dragon gives him something different: family at his side. He has never needed anyone to prove how dangerous he is, but teaming with Dralístico turns his confidence into something sharper. This is not just a reunion of brothers. It is a statement that bloodline, pride, and lucha tradition still matter in a tag division full of chaos.

Dragon Lee brings the cleaner edge of the team: fast combinations, explosive counters, and the discipline to strike at exactly the right second. In OWL, he is not here to live off reputation. He is here to show that even a dragon is more dangerous when it is not flying alone.

name: Dralistico
whyImHere:
Dralístico walks into OWL with the swagger of a man who knows exactly where he comes from and exactly what his family name means. Flashy, dangerous, and impossible to fully control, he brings the wild side of lucha libre to Double Dragon. Where Dragon Lee attacks with precision, Dralístico attacks with flair, risk, and attitude.

This team gives him more than a partner. It gives him a stage to prove that he is not standing in anyone’s shadow—not even his brother’s. Dralístico is here to turn every match into a spectacle, every mistake into an opening, and every opponent into another reminder that family pride can be violent when tested. In OWL, he is not just teaming with Dragon Lee. He is helping unleash both dragons at once.

name: Sheamus Farrelly
whyImHere:
Sheamus has never needed luck, but he has always understood a fight. Built on barroom toughness, veteran instincts, and a career full of championship scars, Sheamus came to OWL looking for the kind of violence that keeps a man sharp. He has done the big moments, the bright lights, and the title runs. Now he wants something meaner: a tag division that can actually test him.

That is where Jordan Devlin comes in. Sheamus saw the same Irish edge in him — pride, anger, skill, and just enough spite to make people uncomfortable. Together, Hard Luck is not about friendship or nostalgia. It is about punishment. Sheamus brings the heavy hands, the veteran cruelty, and the reminder that if you step across from him, you better be ready for a proper scrap.

name: Jordan Devlin
whyImHere:
Jordan Devlin has spent too long being overlooked, renamed, underestimated, or treated like somebody else’s side story. In OWL, he gets to be exactly what he is: a dangerous Irish technician with a chip on his shoulder and no interest in playing nice. Devlin is sharp, bitter, talented, and always one clean opening away from making someone regret dismissing him.

Teaming with Sheamus gives him muscle, credibility, and a partner who understands that Irish pride is not just something you wave around — it is something you swing like a weapon. Devlin brings the precision to Hard Luck, picking opponents apart while Sheamus breaks them down. He is not here to be carried by a legend. He is here to prove he belongs beside one.

name: Rob Van Dam
whyImHere:
Rob Van Dam has never needed to explain himself. He walks into OWL with the same calm confidence that made him one of wrestling’s most original stars: relaxed, dangerous, and always one kick away from changing the whole match. RVD does not chase trends, and he definitely does not force chemistry. He just vibes, fights, flies, and lets the crowd catch up.

That is why Matt Riddle somehow makes perfect sense. RVD sees the same laid-back confidence in him, but with a different kind of edge. Where Riddle brings barefoot combat and MMA pressure, RVD brings veteran timing, educated feet, and highlight-reel creativity. Together, Dude…Bro is not trying to be normal. They are trying to be smooth, unpredictable, and impossible to prepare for.

name: Matt Riddle
whyImHere:
Matt Riddle came to OWL with bare feet, big confidence, and no interest in overthinking anything. He fights like a man who can smile through a conversation and then suddenly fold someone in half. That easygoing attitude makes people underestimate him, but Riddle’s background makes the truth pretty simple: relaxed does not mean harmless.

Teaming with Rob Van Dam gives Riddle the perfect partner. RVD understands rhythm, timing, and controlled chaos better than almost anyone. Riddle brings grappling, strikes, and that “bro, I got this” energy that either drives opponents crazy or gets them knocked out. Dude…Bro works because neither man is pretending. They are chill, confident, dangerous, and fully capable of winning a match before anyone realizes things got serious.

name: Mistico
nickname: El Príncipe de Plata y Oro (The Prince of Silver and Gold)
whyImHere:
Místico enters OWL carrying the glow of lucha libre royalty. He is not just fast, graceful, or spectacular — he wrestles like the mask itself has history behind it. Every entrance feels ceremonial, every dive feels effortless, and every opponent quickly learns that beauty in motion can still be deadly.

Teaming with Máscara Dorada gives Místico a partner who understands the same language: flight, rhythm, honor, and gold. But Místico is not here just to inspire respect. He is here to remind the tag division that legacy still matters when it can still move faster than everyone else. In Ídolos de Oro, Místico brings the myth, the aura, and the veteran brilliance that turns a match into a showcase.

name: Mascara Dorada
whyImHere:
Máscara Dorada is the golden flash of Ídolos de Oro — young, fearless, and built to make impossible movement look natural. He carries himself like someone who knows lucha libre is more than a style. It is art, risk, pride, and pressure all happening at full speed.

Standing beside Místico gives Dorada a chance to prove he is not just the next great flyer. He is ready to stand beside greatness and add his own name to the story. Where Místico brings legend and control, Dorada brings explosion and hunger. OWL gives him a bigger stage, brighter lights, and faster competition. That is exactly what he wants. If the tag division blinks, Dorada will already be in the air.

name: Samoa Joe
whyImHere:
Samoa Joe has never needed a famous family name to make people fear him. His reputation was built the hard way: violence, pressure, precision, and the kind of cold confidence that makes every opponent feel trapped before the first strike lands. Joe does not chase validation from bloodlines, legends, or industry politics. He is the proof.

In The Forgotten Family, Joe stands as the elder threat — the man who has seen every style, survived every war, and still walks like the room belongs to him. He knows what it feels like to be respected but not always celebrated in the same breath as wrestling’s famous Samoan dynasty. That disrespect becomes fuel. Joe is not here to ask for recognition. He is here to choke it out of OWL.

name: Toa Liona
whyImHere:
Toa Liona carries island power without needing anyone else’s shadow attached to it. He is massive, violent, explosive, and built like something that should come with a warning siren. Where other men talk about heritage, Toa turns his into impact. Every charge, every strike, every collision feels like a reminder that Samoan dominance did not come from one family tree.

Teaming with Samoa Joe gives Toa a dangerous kind of direction. Joe brings the strategy, cruelty, and veteran control. Toa brings the raw force that makes plans unnecessary when bodies start flying. In The Forgotten Family, Toa is not the younger man waiting his turn. He is the unleashed consequence. OWL wanted monsters. Toa Liona came to remind them some monsters were ignored too long.

name: Bron Breakker
whyImHere:
Bron Breakker does not enter a tag division looking for chemistry. He enters looking for something to run through. Built like a linebacker, wired like a guard dog, and always one bark away from turning a match into a collision, Bron brings pure explosive violence to What Da Dog Doin’.

He is not subtle, and he is not trying to be. Bron’s whole game is pressure: sprint, smash, suplex, repeat. But teaming with Clark Connors gives that chaos a partner who understands the same language. They are both intense, both physical, and both carry themselves like the fight started before the bell. Bron is the bigger, louder warning sign of the team — if you hear the bark, the bite is already coming.

name: Clark Connors
whyImHere:
Clark Connors is all snarl, grit, and bad intentions. He does not wrestle pretty, and he does not care if anybody enjoys the process. Connors fights like a man who would rather drag an opponent into deep water than put on a clean performance. He is rough, stubborn, aggressive, and proud of every ugly second.

That makes Bron Breakker a perfect kind of problem for him. In What Da Dog Doin’, Connors is not standing beside some calm technician trying to balance him out. He is standing beside another unleashed animal. Bron brings the explosive power. Connors brings the mean streak, the grind, and the nasty follow-through. Together, they are not here to outclass teams. They are here to maul them.

name: Axiom
whyImHere:
Axiom brings mystery, precision, and quiet brilliance to every match. Behind the mask is not just a high-flyer, but a thinker — someone who sees wrestling like angles, timing, and openings waiting to be solved. Where Nathan Frazer moves like lightning, Axiom moves like calculation wrapped in speed.

That contrast is what makes Fraxiom work. Axiom does not need Frazer to calm down completely; he just needs to aim him. Their team became known for balancing tension with chemistry, even with WWE framing them at times as partners who were not always on the same page while still defending championship gold.

In OWL, Axiom is the structure beneath the chaos. He makes the impossible look planned.

name: Nathan Frazer
whyImHere:
Nathan Frazer wrestles like someone hit fast-forward and forgot to turn it off. He is speed, risk, reaction, and chaos packed into one body, constantly pushing the pace until opponents start making mistakes just trying to keep up. But in Fraxiom, that reckless energy finally found a purpose.

Frazer and Axiom started as rivals before realizing their styles could become something much more dangerous together. WWE’s own profile notes that their unforgettable trilogy in NXT sparked the idea of joining forces, and Fraxiom went on to become NXT Tag Team Champions.

In OWL, Frazer is the accelerator. He brings the snap, the dives, the counters, and the refusal to slow down. He does not want a normal tag match. He wants a sprint.

name: Mark Davis
finisher: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND COUNT TO FUCK
whyImHere:
Mark Davis walks into OWL like a man who already knows the ring is too small for him. He is not here to impress people with speeches, flash, or empty threats. Davis brings heavy hands, nasty power, and the kind of tag-team experience that makes him dangerous before the match even starts.

In Southern Cross, Davis is the bruising half of the storm — the man who cuts off the ring, crushes momentum, and makes every opponent feel like they just got trapped in a pub fight with no exits. He may not always be the loudest man in the room, but once the bell rings, he makes himself impossible to ignore. OWL’s tag division is full of teams trying to prove they belong. Davis is here to prove they should have stayed out of his way.

name: Bronson Reed
whyImHere:
Bronson Reed is not built to blend in. He is built to flatten people. A super-heavyweight wrecking machine with terrifying speed for his size, Reed brings a different kind of pressure to OWL: the kind where opponents know what is coming and still cannot stop it.

Teaming with Mark Davis gives Reed exactly what he needs — another big, mean Australian who understands that some matches do not need to be pretty. In Southern Cross, Reed is the avalanche. He overwhelms, crushes, and ends conversations with impact. Every splash, collision, and body drop feels like the ring itself is being punished. Bronson Reed did not come to OWL looking for opportunity. He came looking for bodies to stack.

name: Hollowell
whyImHere:
Hollowell does not talk much, and somehow that makes her worse. Nobody knows the full truth about where she came from, only the rumors: locked rooms, years of silence, a childhood people whisper about but never explain out loud. She was not burned, but something in her past left marks deeper than fire ever could.

When Hollowell walks into OWL, the mood changes. She is not here for fame, friendship, or redemption. She is here because pain is the only language she trusts, and now she finally gets to speak it back. Cold, towering, and almost impossible to read, Hollowell treats every match like punishment being delivered late.

They call her “The Woman from Hell” because once she steps through the ropes, it feels like something followed her in.

name: Mikayla Lashawn
whyImHere:
Mikayla LaShawn was born for the spotlight and acts like the spotlight should be thankful. Flashy, loud, dramatic, and dangerous when the bell rings, “The Heartbreak Chick” brings main-character energy to every entrance, every pose, and every superkick she throws.

Mikayla grew up studying the icons who could steal a show with one look, one strut, and one perfectly timed kick to the jaw. But she is not here to cosplay greatness — she is here to remix it. She has the confidence of a woman who knows the crowd is watching, the attitude of someone who expects flowers before she earns them, and the talent to make people mad that she might actually be right.

Win or lose, Mikayla LaShawn makes sure the match is about her. And if you blink, heartbreak comes quick.

name: Catalina Velez
whyImHere:
Catalina Velez did not come to OWL to be admired. She came to be obeyed. Known as “La Reina Roja” — The Red Queen, Catalina carries herself like royalty, but there is nothing soft or ceremonial about her rule. Every step is controlled, every word is measured, and every attack feels like a sentence being handed down.

Raised in Colombia, Catalina learned early that power is not given to the loudest person in the room. It belongs to the one who can stay calm while everyone else panics. In the ring, she wrestles with cold confidence: sharp counters, punishing strikes, and a cruel patience that makes opponents feel like they are slowly being cornered.

Catalina does not chase the throne. She walks in already wearing the crown.

name: Raven Kaine
whyImHere:
Raven Kaine was built in Manchester, where punk noise, street fights, and survival all bleed into the same rhythm. Raised between her father’s boxing gym behind a pub and the rough corners of London’s underground scene, Raven learned early that toughness was not a personality trait. It was armor.

She was the kid teachers called a problem, bullies learned not to test, and crowds eventually could not look away from. At sixteen, she found British wrestling and saw everything she already understood: violence, theatre, rebellion, and control. From there, she sharpened herself through catch wrestling, Muay Thai, and raw street-fight instincts.

Raven does not wrestle clean, pretty, or polite. She fights like every match is a warning shot. In OWL, she is not here to be accepted. She is here to make the room uncomfortable.

name: Ayjay Sparks
whyImHere: Hailing from Orlando, Florida, Ayjay Sparks is pure motion—an explosive athlete who seems to move faster the brighter the spotlight gets. She built her name on speed, spring, and fearless aerial attacks, turning every match into a showcase of creativity and controlled chaos. Her butterfly imagery isn’t just for style; to Ayjay, butterflies represent growth, freedom, and transformation. She sees herself the same way—a wrestler who refuses to stay trapped in one box and keeps evolving every time people think they’ve figured her out. That mindset comes to life in Flutter Shock, her breathtaking backflipping corner dropkick that can change a match in an instant. Ayjay is here to prove that grace and danger can exist in the same heartbeat—and she plans to rise above the entire division.

name: Katsumi Nanami
whyImHere:
Katsumi Nanami looks like someone who wandered out of a cosplay convention and accidentally became dangerous. From South Korea, she built her identity around cute chaos: wolf ears, catlike confidence, playful smiles, and gear that makes opponents think she is more mascot than menace. That mistake usually lasts about thirty seconds.

Behind the sweet look is a sharp, instinctive wrestler who moves like prey until she decides to become the predator. Katsumi blends speed, flexibility, sudden counters, and a mean little bite when opponents get too comfortable. She loves the fun, the fantasy, and the performance of her wolf/cat persona, but none of it is a joke.

Katsumi smiles because she knows something her opponent does not: cute can still claw your eyes out.

name: Shonda Shamrock
whyImHere:
Shonda Shamrock was raised with one rule: if you step into a fight, finish it. Carrying the Shamrock name means carrying a legacy of intensity, discipline, and controlled violence, but Shonda is not here to live off someone else’s reputation. She is here to make the name even meaner.

Nicknamed “Savage,” Shonda blends old-school shoot-fighting toughness with modern MMA explosiveness. She can strike, grapple, throw, and submit opponents before they realize the match has stopped being wrestling and turned into survival. There is a little Ken Shamrock in her rage, a little Ronda Rousey in her confidence, and a whole lot of her own attitude in how quickly she can ruin somebody’s night.

Shonda does not want respect handed down. She wants it tapped out.

name: Sami Deddley
whyImHere:
Little Sami Deddley is the smallest member of the family, which only means she has farther to fly before crashing into somebody. Born and raised in Deddleyville, USA, Sami grew up surrounded by broken tables, louder relatives, and the understanding that being underestimated is practically a family tradition.

She does not have Martha May’s size or Chevonne’s smoothness, so Sami survives through speed, recklessness, and a complete lack of concern for her own safety. She will launch herself from anywhere, absorb punishment that should keep her down, and come back smiling like pain was part of the plan.

As the singles representative of The Deddley Sistaz, Sami carries the family name into every fight alone. She may be little, but chaos has never needed much room.

name: Martha May Deddley
whyImHere:
Martha May Deddley is the loudest voice in Deddleyville and usually the reason furniture starts moving. Big, powerful, and permanently one bad look away from violence, Martha May leads with attitude before backing it up with brute force.

She treats the ring like a family living room during an argument: everybody is invited, nothing is safe, and somebody is probably going through a table. Martha May brings the power to her partnership with Chevonne, cutting opponents off, throwing them around, and making sure every match becomes the kind of mess the Deddleys understand best.

She does not care about being graceful, respectable, or easy to manage. Martha May is here to make The Deddley Sistaz impossible to ignore—and to remind OWL that Deddleyville raises women who hit harder than most men.

name: Chevonne Deddley
whyImHere:
Chevonne Deddley is the smoother half of the family operation, but “smooth” does not mean safe. She moves with more rhythm and precision than Martha May, striking fast, changing direction, and making opponents forget that the next impact might still involve a table.

Growing up in Deddleyville, USA, Chevonne learned how to survive chaos without getting swallowed by it. She became the one who could see the opening, control the pace, and turn Martha May’s destruction into something coordinated. Where Martha overwhelms, Chevonne redirects. Where Martha breaks the door down, Chevonne is already inside.

As one-half of the Deddley tag team, Chevonne brings balance to the madness. She may look like the calmer sister, but that only means she enjoys setting the trap before the whole room explodes.

name: Brigitte Krauss
whyImHere:
Brigitte Krauss does not consider professional wrestling entertainment. To her, it is a sacred discipline corrupted by performers more interested in applause than mastery. Raised in Vienna, Austria, “The Iron Empress” built herself through punishing European training, classical grappling, and the belief that the squared circle must be treated with absolute respect.

Krauss enters every match determined to restore order through violence. Her chops sound like gunshots, her throws punish poor technique, and her submissions force opponents to either acknowledge her superiority or lose consciousness resisting it. She has no patience for theatrics, shortcuts, or wrestlers who treat the mat like a stage.

Brigitte does not need the crowd’s approval. She demands obedience. Inside her ring, there are only two choices: respect the mat—or get choked out.

name: Kaijin
whyImHere:
Kaijin came out of Osaka’s underground fight scene with the rhythm of an artist and the cruelty of an executioner. She treats strong style like a performance—every sway, pause, and crooked smile designed to make opponents hesitate before the storm finally hits.

Known as “The Storm Made Flesh,” Kaijin is unpredictable by choice. She may circle the ring like she is dancing, then suddenly drive a knee through someone’s jaw with frightening precision. Her strikes are heavy, her timing is vicious, and her confidence borders on mockery. She enjoys making opponents feel foolish almost as much as she enjoys knocking them unconscious.

Kaijin did not come to OWL seeking respect. She believes respect is what remains after fear has done its work.

name: Fatima Okoye
whyImHere: Fatima Okoye fights like a campaign, not a contest. Raised in the Republic of the Congo, she grew up in an environment where discipline, readiness, and mental toughness meant survival. That shaped her into a cold, highly controlled competitor who sees the ring as a battlefield and every opponent as a target to be broken down. Fatima does not play to the crowd, show emotion, or waste movement. She marches forward, cuts off escape, and punishes mistakes with ruthless efficiency. Her stern presence alone makes people uneasy, but once the bell rings, the real danger begins. She calls herself The One-Woman Militia because she does not need backup, allies, or approval—only an objective. In OWL, her mission is simple: impose order through violence and make the entire division fear her name.

name: Madison Monroe McMahon
whyImHere: Madison Monroe McMahon was born into wrestling royalty, and she carries herself like she owns every building she walks into. The daughter of Triple H and Stephanie McMahon, Triple M grew up around power, politics, and pressure, so she learned early that second place is for people without a legacy to protect. She wrestles with the same calculated cruelty and ring IQ as her father, picking opponents apart before finishing them with the Birthright, her own devastating version of the Pedigree. Triple M doesn’t just want to win—she wants to rule. Cold, cerebral, arrogant, and always in control, she believes OWL isn’t a proving ground for her, it’s an inheritance waiting to be claimed. To her, dominance isn’t earned. It’s family tradition.

name: Lessana Brokova
whyImHere: Lessana Brokova is raw force turned into a woman. Raised in Almaty with a background in combat sports and strength training, she built her reputation the hard way—by ragdolling opponents, breaking their confidence, and leaving no doubt who the strongest woman in the fight was. She doesn’t care about showmanship, popularity, or respect. She cares about domination. Every match is treated like a hunt, and once she gets her hands on someone, escape becomes nearly impossible. Brokova fights with the cold, brutal efficiency of a predator, throwing bodies with terrifying ease before ending them with Kazakh Kontrol or the devastating Tornado F5. She isn’t here to outwrestle the division. She’s here to overwhelm it, conquer it, and make everyone else feel small.

name: Annabelle Coven
whyImHere: From Salem, Massachusetts, Annabelle Coven carries herself like a curse given human form. Raised on old family stories, ritual discipline, and the belief that power belongs to those bold enough to seize it, she entered wrestling with the same purpose her ancestors trained into her: dominate, collect, and never apologize. Draped in black and moving with cold confidence, Annabelle treats the ring like sacred ground and everyone inside it like a sacrifice waiting to happen. She doesn’t rant or beg for attention—her presence does the work for her. Every stare feels like a warning, every step feels like bad luck arriving early. She calls herself “The Dark Angel” because she doesn’t come to save anyone—she comes to judge them. And when she lands Fallen Angel or traps someone in Covenant, it’s already too late. Annabelle Coven isn’t chasing gold. She believes it already belongs to her.

name: Jayme Toxic
whyImHere: Raised in the heart of Germany’s punk and rock scene, Jayme Toxic built her name by turning attitude into an art form and chaos into a calling card. A viral social-media wrestling sensation and hardened indie ass-kicker, J-Tox mixes rebellious energy with real toughness, backing up every bit of hype the second the bell rings. She comes to the ring like a headliner, air-guitaring to the crowd and feeding off the noise, but once the match starts she fights with sharp aggression, fearless speed, and a refusal to stay down. For Jayme, wrestling is loud, raw, and meant to make people feel something. She’s here to shake up OWL, outwork the doubters, and drop anyone in her way with The Detox, her cradle-style DDT that can end the party in an instant.

name: Valentina Calderón
whyImHere:
Valentina Calderón carries herself like she was born to shine under the brightest lights. Known as La Heredera, she wears a crown on her mask as a symbol that lucha greatness runs through her blood and that she intends to honor that legacy, not coast on it. To Valentina, being wrestling royalty is not about privilege—it is about responsibility, pride, and representing her people with courage.

Inside the ring, she combines elegance with explosiveness, moving with the confidence of a queen and the urgency of a fighter who knows every match is a chance to defend her name. Her style is fast, fearless, and dramatic, capped off by La Maldición Real, a sudden corner flip cutter that can end a match in an instant. Valentina is here to prove that the next great luchadora icon wears a crown.

name: Liberty Van Horn
whyImHere:
Liberty Van Horn was born into expectation. A second-generation superstar from a family built on championships, Olympic discipline, and public greatness, Liberty grew up knowing the Van Horn name opened doors—but it also made every failure louder. Her father was a decorated champion and Olympian. Her brother carried the family standard after him. Now Liberty steps into OWL determined to prove the legacy did not skip her.

Known as “The Torch of Freedom,” Liberty wrestles with prestige, confidence, and just enough arrogance to remind everyone that she believes greatness is in her blood. She is polished, powerful, and brutally competitive, mixing elite athletic fundamentals with a champion’s sense of timing. When she hits The Liberty Torch, her devastating clothesline, it feels less like a move and more like a family statement.

Liberty is not here to follow the Van Horn legacy. She is here to raise it higher.

name: Maxxine Jaqueline Freeman
whyImHere:
Maxxine Jacqueline Freeman was not built in struggle. She was built in privilege, applause, and the absolute belief that she was born better than everyone else. From expensive training schools to private coaches and carefully curated spotlight moments, Maxxine has spent her entire life being told she was special—and somehow, she still thinks people are underestimating her.

She talks fast, insults faster, and carries herself like OWL should be grateful she bothered to show up. Maxxine does not just want to win matches; she wants to embarrass opponents, manipulate the room, and remind every fan that their boos still count as attention. Under the designer attitude and smug smile, though, is a dangerous technician who knows exactly when to cheat, when to bait, and when to strike.

Maxxine Jacqueline Freeman is rich, ruthless, and convinced she is the future. The worst part? She might be right.

name: Kristy Grimm
whyImHere: Kristy Grimm doesn’t come from a hometown so much as a warning label—billed from Every Man’s Nightmare, she arrived in OWL carrying the chaos of the death-match underground with her. Known as “Princess Death,” Kristy blends goth glamour, death-metal attitude, and a dangerous edge that makes her impossible to look away from. There’s a dark seduction to her presence, but beneath the eyeliner and attitude is someone who built her name by surviving violence most wrestlers would run from. She used to thrive in blood-soaked mayhem, but OWL gives her a bigger stage to prove she’s more than a death-match spectacle. With her brutal Just Die a Little cave-in stomp, Kristy wants to show that beauty can be deadly—and that nightmares belong in championship contention.

name: Dallis Goodnight
whyImHere: Dallis Goodnight was once a rising competitive cheerleader, traveling the country and building her life around perfect timing, fearless athleticism, and the spotlight. That dream nearly ended when a brutal fall tore her ACL and left her facing a long recovery, surgeries, and a metal plate in her knee. During that dark stretch, wrestling gave her something cheer never could—an outlet for pain, grit, and reinvention. Instead of going back to the mat to perform, she stepped into the ring to fight. Now known as “The Super Starlette,” Dallis blends natural showmanship with hard-earned toughness, turning her biggest setback into her deadliest weapon. The same knee that almost ended her career now puts opponents down for good with Say Goodnight.

name: Lena Kross
whyImHere:
Lena Kross arrives in OWL with size, presence, and the kind of confidence that makes a division feel smaller the second she walks in. The Australian powerhouse has already been presented in AEW as a major threat, with AEW listing her as part of its women’s roster and a Women’s World Tag Team Champion, while F4W noted her arrival at Grand Slam Australia and her alignment with Megan Bayne.

In OWL, Kross is here to prove she is more than muscle beside someone else. She is a singles problem: imposing, cold, and built to punish anyone who mistakes her silence for hesitation. Lena does not need flash when force works faster. Every match is a chance to make the ring feel like her territory—and every opponent feel like they wandered into it by mistake.

name: Alice Crowley
whyImHere:
Alice Crowley brings indie-fight toughness into OWL with the kind of confidence that feels earned, not manufactured. Known as “Big Al,” Crowley has built her name through gritty matches, intergender experience, and a willingness to throw herself into uncomfortable fights instead of waiting for perfect opportunities. Her work has been tracked across the independent scene, with profiles listing her nickname and Indianapolis roots, while recent coverage has tied her to JCW and noted her as a former JCW Women’s Champion.

In OWL, Big Al is here to be the problem nobody booked around. She is scrappy, loud when she needs to be, and tougher than opponents expect. Crowley does not need polish to be dangerous; she just needs one opening and enough attitude to make it hurt.

name: Persephone
whyImHere:
Persephone walks into OWL with the aura of someone who already believes the ring belongs beneath her feet. Inspired by the queen of the underworld, she wrestles with a mix of lucha confidence, power offense, and cold arrogance that makes her feel more like a final boss than a rising contender. Her road through CMLL sharpened her into a dangerous presence, with major championship success proving she can thrive when the lights are brightest.

In OWL, Persephone is not here to be another luchadora on the roster. She is here to rule the division from the shadows, drag opponents into deep water, and remind everyone that beauty and brutality can wear the same face. When Persephone takes control, the match stops feeling like competition and starts feeling like a descent.

name: Stephanie Vaquer
whyImHere:
Stephanie Vaquer arrives in OWL carrying international credibility and a style that feels sharp, violent, and impossible to ignore. WWE bills her from San Fernando, Chile, lists Devil’s Kiss, SVB, and Vaquer Inferno among her signature moves, and describes her as a hard-hitting champion who has made an impact across Japan and the Americas.

In OWL, Vaquer is here to bring that same cold precision to a new battlefield. She does not need to be the loudest woman in the room because every strike, counter, and stare says enough. Her presence feels elegant, but her offense is nasty: calculated pressure, sudden impact, and that dangerous confidence of someone who knows she belongs at the top.

Stephanie Vaquer is not chasing validation. La Primera is here to remind OWL that history follows her wherever she goes.

name: Giulia
whyImHere:
Giulia brings OWL the kind of presence that feels international, dangerous, and instantly main-event level. WWE lists her hometown as Tokyo, Japan, with career highlights including NXT Women’s Champion, 2024 Women’s Iron Survivor, and Women’s United States Champion. WWE also describes her as having brutal strikes, undeniable charisma, and an electric presence.

In OWL, Giulia is here to turn elegance into violence. She does not need to scream for attention; the danger is in how calmly she walks toward it. Every strike feels deliberate, every stare feels like a warning, and every match carries that “Beautiful Madness” edge—controlled until the exact second it becomes chaos. Giulia is not coming in as a guest star. She is coming in like a woman who expects the entire division to adjust around her.

name: Roxanne Perez
whyImHere:
Roxanne Perez enters OWL with the résumé of someone who grew up obsessed with wrestling and then somehow became ahead of schedule anyway. WWE bills her from Laredo, Texas, lists Pop Rox as her signature move, and notes her history as an NXT Women’s Champion, NXT Women’s Tag Team Champion, first-ever Women’s Breakout Tournament winner, and first-ever Women’s Iron Survivor Challenge winner.

In OWL, Roxanne is here to prove “The Prodigy” is not just a nickname. She brings speed, timing, emotion, and a scrappy edge that makes bigger opponents look flat-footed. She can wrestle from underneath, fire back with heart, and turn one opening into a match-ending moment. Roxanne may look young, but she fights like someone who already knows exactly where she belongs: near the top.

name: Asuka
whyImHere:
Asuka enters OWL as controlled chaos in human form: colorful, unpredictable, and mean enough to make even the toughest opponents second-guess themselves. Billed from Osaka, Japan, she brings world-class technique, wicked striking, and the dangerous smile of someone who enjoys every second of punishment she creates. WWE lists the Asuka Lock as her signature move and notes major accomplishments including NXT Women’s Champion, Raw Women’s Champion, SmackDown Women’s Champion, WWE Women’s Champion, Women’s Tag Team Champion, first-ever Women’s Royal Rumble winner, and Money in the Bank winner.

In OWL, Asuka is not here to prove she belongs. She already knows. She is here to poison the division with fear, stretch people past their limits, and remind everyone of the same truth: nobody is ready for Asuka.

name: Athena
whyImHere:
Athena enters OWL with champion energy and a mean streak that does not need warming up. AEW lists her as the ROH Women’s World Champion, from Dallas, Texas, with The O-Face as her signature move.

In OWL, Athena is here to run the division like a throne room with turnbuckles. She brings power, speed, arrogance, and that nasty “prove me wrong” edge that makes every match feel personal. Athena does not just beat opponents; she makes examples out of them, forcing everyone to acknowledge that the Fallen Goddess is not descending to their level.

She is not arriving to fit into OWL’s women’s division. She is arriving to rule over it.

name: Willow Nightingale
whyImHere:
Willow Nightingale brings the kind of energy OWL needs: bright smile, big heart, and enough power to fold somebody in half. AEW lists her on its women’s roster, and her reputation has been built around that rare mix of warmth and danger — the kind of wrestler fans want to cheer for, but opponents absolutely hate seeing across the ring.

In OWL, Willow is here to prove joy is not weakness. She can dance her way to the ring, light up the crowd, and still launch someone with a suplex like she is throwing out bad vibes by force. Her powerbomb-based offense makes every match feel like one mistake could end the night, but what really separates Willow is that she fights like someone who believes the whole room can rise with her.

Willow Nightingale is not coming to OWL to dim herself down. She is bringing sunshine, violence, and championship-level momentum with her.

name: Tatum Paxley
whyImHere:
Tatum Paxley brings OWL a presence that feels sweet for about three seconds—then deeply, deeply wrong. WWE bills her from Dallas, Texas, lists her as a former NXT Women’s Champion and NXT Women’s North American Champion, and describes her as an outcast who fully embraced her misfit side, complete with creepy dolls and unsettling fixations on other wrestlers.

In OWL, Tatum is here to make every match feel uncomfortable before it becomes dangerous. She smiles when she should be nervous, studies opponents like collectibles, and turns obsession into strategy. Under the strange behavior is a sharp, violent competitor who found power in being different. Tatum does not want to fit in anymore. She wants the division to adjust to her weird little world.

name: La Catalina
whyImHere:
La Catalina brings OWL a star presence shaped by lucha, glamour, and international pressure. A Chilean wrestler who has competed under names including Catalina/Carolina and La Catalina, she rebuilt herself after earlier WWE experience and became one of CMLL’s standout Amazonas, including winning the Universal de Amazonas tournament.

In OWL, Catalina is here to prove she is not just a beautiful entrance, a big-match smile, or another name from the lucha world. She is a polished, confident performer with enough attitude to make every opponent feel like they are standing across from someone destined for a spotlight. Catalina wrestles like a woman who knows the camera loves her—but knows victory looks even better.

name: Bayley
whyImHere:
Bayley comes to OWL with one of the most complete résumés in women’s wrestling: NXT Women’s Champion, Raw Women’s Champion, SmackDown Women’s Champion, WWE Women’s Champion, Women’s Tag Team Champion, Money in the Bank winner, and 2024 Royal Rumble winner. WWE bills her from San Jose, California and lists Bayley-to-Belly and Rose Plant as signature moves.

In OWL, Bayley is here as a veteran who has already lived every version of herself: the beloved superfan, the ruthless champion, the locker room leader, and the woman who can stand alone after betrayal. She brings timing, toughness, and a mind for the game that makes every opponent feel like they are wrestling someone three steps ahead.

Bayley is not here to restart. She is here to remind everyone that a true Role Model adapts, survives, and wins.

name: Tiffany Omari Porter
whyImHere:
Tiffany Omari Porter was raised to believe that the family name was not something you inherited for free—it was something you protected in public. As the older, sharper, and more polished daughter of MVP, T.O.P. carries herself like the boardroom already voted in her favor before the match even started.

Tiffany studied the game from the luxury box and the locker room, learning how her father turned violence into enterprise. She is calculated, composed, and brutally selective with her attacks. She does not waste movement, words, or opportunities. Every entrance feels like a press conference. Every match feels like a hostile takeover.

T.O.P. is not trying to prove she belongs in wrestling. She is trying to prove the business belongs to her.

name: Victoria Imani Porter
whyImHere:
Victoria Imani Porter grew up around power, but unlike Tiffany, she never cared about sounding polished. V.I.P. is flashier, louder, and quicker to turn confidence into disrespect. She inherited MVP’s swagger, but added her own social-media-age arrogance: designer shades, expensive attitude, and the belief that every camera in the building should be pointed at her.

Victoria is the risk-taker of the sisters. She talks big, moves fast, and loves embarrassing opponents before putting them away. Underneath the show is a dangerous competitor who knows how to bait people into mistakes and capitalize before they realize they’ve been played.

V.I.P. does not just want access to the top floor. She wants the velvet rope removed, the spotlight centered, and everyone else left waiting outside.

name: Kim McCratt
whyImHere: Kim McCratt fights like a campaign speech with a time limit and a hidden blade. A lifelong debater with a polished public image, she built her reputation on being smarter, quicker, and more prepared than everyone in the room. In the ring, that turns into relentless pressure, sharp technique, and a style built around outmaneuvering opponents before they even know they’re trapped. She presents herself as the voice of reason, but there’s nothing soft about her once the bell rings. Kim believes progress only comes through force, and if she has to drag the division forward by its hair, so be it. With the Pennsylvania Avenue Plunge (moonsault German suplex) and her Finisher, Checks n Balances, she proves that policy and punishment can go hand in hand.

name: Connie Servativv
whyImHere: Connie Servativv carries herself like order in human form—disciplined, severe, and absolutely convinced she’s right. She doesn’t waste motion, words, or sympathy. Connie believes the world has gotten too comfortable, and she sees wrestling as the perfect place to restore consequences. Her style is grounded, punishing, and brutally efficient, built around wearing people down and breaking their base piece by piece. Once she gets hold of a leg or plants someone with The Filibuster, the fight is usually over. Her signature hold, Make Ankles Break Again, says everything you need to know about her mindset. Connie doesn’t care if the crowd agrees with her politics. She only cares that, by the end of the match, everybody understands her authority.

name: Keva Owensteen
whyImHere: Keva Owensteen is the sister who does not believe wrestling should be pretty. She believes it should hurt. Raised on grainy indie tapes, parking-lot brawls, blood feuds, and the kind of violence polite companies pretend never existed, Keva built herself into a walking warning sign. She is the nastier half of the Owensteen sisters: reckless, mouthy, fearless, and completely willing to win ugly. Keva does not care about respect, rankings, or how the match looks on paper. She cares about impact. She fights like every opponent personally insulted her family, and once the crowd starts chanting along with her chaos, she becomes even harder to control. Keva does not enter OWL to compete. She enters to wreck the room.

name: Kayla Owensteen
whyImHere: Kayla Owensteen is the cleaner presentation with the same dangerous family wiring. Where Keva is pure destruction, Kayla is the prizefighter: sharper, colder, and always thinking three cheap shots ahead. She knows when to talk, when to stall, when to bait an opponent into swinging first, and when to make the referee look the wrong way. Kayla built her identity around proving that toughness is not just about who hits hardest—it is about who survives longest and leaves with the win. She can play to the crowd, mock an opponent, and turn a match around with one perfectly timed strike. Kayla may look more controlled than her sister, but underneath the smirk is the same Owensteen problem: violence with purpose.

name: Jess Harvey
