whyImHere: Jess Harvey is the wild heartbeat of the Harvey Girlz. Also from Cameron, North Carolina, Jess grew up chasing adrenaline, living for risk, and turning chaos into art. If Katt is the architect, Jess is the daredevil spark that makes the whole thing explode into something unforgettable. She throws herself into every fight with reckless passion, trusting instinct, heart, and pure Xtreme energy. Jess doesn’t care about playing it smart if playing it bold gets the crowd on their feet. She fights like she has something to prove every second she’s in the ring, and that makes her dangerous. To Jess, OWL is the perfect stage to show the world that the Harvey Girlz weren’t made to blend in — they were made to fly.

name: Katt Harvey
whyImHere: From Cameron, North Carolina, Katt Harvey grew up turning backyards, barns, and anything with a ledge into her personal proving ground. As the older sister, she became the brain of the Harvey Girlz — the one always dreaming bigger, fighting harder, and pushing the team toward the next impossible stunt. Katt carries that same never-say-die spirit into OWL, mixing toughness, creativity, and fearless aggression every time she steps through the ropes. She doesn’t just want to win matches — she wants to make people remember them. For Katt, wrestling has always been about rebellion, passion, and leaving your mark in a world that wants you to play it safe. The Harvey Girlz were born to do the exact opposite.

name: Stormy Frunts
whyImHere: Stormy Frunts is pure Mississippi confidence wrapped in denim-country charm and knockout force. Raised in Sweetcorn County, Stormy grew up around farms, dusty roads, and no-nonsense people, which made her tough long before she ever stepped into a ring. She first got attention for her larger-than-life personality at the Rusty Nail Gentlemen’s Club, but outside the spotlight she was grinding through the local wrestling scene, learning how to turn confidence into intimidation. Stormy knows exactly what people see when they look at her — and she uses every bit of it to bait opponents into underestimating her. Then, when the bell rings, she drops them on their heads with Bless Ya Heart or bends them up with the Crawdad Clutch. She’s country, cocky, and mean when she has to be.

name: Daisha Dukes
whyImHere: Daisha Dukes is the sharper tongue and scrappier soul of Southern Heat. Also coming out of Sweetcorn County, Daisha made a name for herself by being the kind of woman who can roast you in a promo, slap you in the mouth, and then out-wrestle you for good measure. She works the Rusty Nail beside Stormy, but in the ring she brings a different kind of flavor — quicker, chippier, and more unpredictable. Daisha’s style has a little funk to it, a little hippy weirdness, and a whole lot of irritation for anyone who thinks she’s just the sidekick. She loves getting under people’s skin, talking through the fight, and turning matches messy. If Stormy is the heavy heat, Daisha is the spice that makes it burn. She’s bold, loud, and always ready to stir the pot.

name: Hitokiri Sune
whyImHere:
Hitokiri Sune is the more openly menacing half of Crimson Lotus. She is the team’s blade — cold, direct, and merciless. Sune fights with the kind of calm brutality that makes opponents panic, because she never looks emotional, never gets rattled, and never wastes energy. Her offense is built around vicious kicks, sharp knees, sudden throws, and the kind of follow-up stomps that make it feel like she’s trying to erase people instead of just beat them.

The rumors around Sune paint her as the team’s enforcer: the woman who handles anything that requires intimidation, violence, or a message being sent. She doesn’t posture or grandstand. She just hurts people and lets the silence do the rest. In Crimson Lotus, Hitokiri Sune is the warning sign. If she’s in front of you, it means you’ve already been marked.

name: Reiyu Hojima
whyImHere:
If Sune is the blade, then Reiyu Hojima is the mind guiding it. Hojima is smoother, more patient, and maybe even more dangerous because of it. She carries herself with a chilling poise, almost elegant in the way she moves, but there is nothing graceful about what happens once she traps an opponent. She excels at manipulation-based offense — twisting limbs, baiting mistakes, cutting off escape routes, and setting victims up for Crimson Lotus to finish together.

Hojima gives the team its air of ceremony. She studies people, adapts, and seems to know exactly when panic sets in. There’s an unsettling intelligence to everything she does, like every match was solved before it started. While Sune is the obvious threat, Hojima is the one making sure the trap closes. She doesn’t need to shout to control the room. One look, one gesture, and Crimson Lotus moves.

name: Liv Morgan
whyImHere: Liv Morgan brings OWL the kind of chaos that smiles first and swings second. WWE presents her as a Jersey-born star tied to the Liv Morgan Revenge Tour, with ObLIVion as one of her signature weapons and a career built around surviving, adapting, and turning heartbreak into damage. In Bad Intentions, Liv is the spark: unpredictable, emotional, reckless when she wants to be, and way smarter than people give her credit for. She can play sweet, play wounded, play underestimated — then flip the match the second someone buys the act. Liv does not just want to win in OWL. She wants opponents questioning whether she was ever the victim or the setup all along.

name: Anna Jay
whyImHere: Anna Jay brings a colder kind of danger to OWL. AEW’s profile highlights how she grew from a young wrestler with only a handful of matches into one of the company’s top women’s competitors, with history alongside Tay Melo as part of TayJay. In Bad Intentions, Anna is the control piece: calm, sharp-eyed, and nasty once the match gets personal. She does not waste energy trying to be chaotic because Liv already has that covered. Anna picks the moment, cuts off the ring, and makes opponents feel trapped before they realize the trap was set. She has the presence of someone who does not need to talk loud to be dangerous. One look tells you enough.
